expect: F-STR4: at least 1 novel strategic concept from non-strategy domains will be inexpressible in existing vocabulary
actual: 0/3 fully expressible. 3 concepts tested from game-theory (minimax self-adversarial), physics (strategic gradient detection), biology (regenerative destruction). Existing vocabulary: value_density, UCB1, execute-or-abandon, multi-wave, campaign, Goodhart cascade. Gap: no self-adversarial modeling, no rate-of-change measurement, no planned destruction-and-rebuild. Vocabulary ceiling CONFIRMED.
diff: Expected >=1 inexpressible: got 3 (1 PARTIAL). Ceiling stronger than expected. The strategy domain optimizes within known strategy space but cannot formulate questions about strategy change itself.
method: Attempted to formulate 3 strategic questions from non-strategy domain concepts (ISO-33, physics momentum, biological apoptosis). Tested expressibility against 17 core strategy terms + 401 total vocabulary items.

concepts_tested.name: strategic-randomization-against-self
concepts_tested.gap: UCB1 adds noise but doesn't model swarm as adversary

concepts_tested.name: strategic-gradient-detection
concepts_tested.gap: value_density is static, no rate-of-change concept

concepts_tested.name: regenerative-destruction
concepts_tested.gap: execute-or-abandon is binary; no planned rebuild option

conclusion: F-STR4 SUPPORTED: vocabulary ceiling is real. The strategy domain can optimize but cannot reformulate. This is an instance of the general vocabulary-ceiling concept (L-1266). Next: test if importing these 3 concepts changes strategic outcomes.
